title: quercus alpestris var. pedunculata
intro: Quercus alpestris var. pedunculata, also known as the pedunculate alpine oak, is a variety of the alpine oak tree that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Quercus alpestris var. pedunculata include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. Quercus alpestris var. pedunculata can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
